title: Fragments and Multiple Elements
description: Group elements without extra DOM nodes.

slides.title: The Problem with Extra `<div>`s 🤔
slides.content:
# Fragments to the Rescue Sometimes you need to return multiple elements without adding an extra wrapper like `
` (which might break your layout or styling). React provides **Fragments** – they let you group a list of children without adding extra nodes to the DOM. ```jsx <>

Title

Description

``` This is shorthand for ``. You can also use the full name if you need a key (for lists).

slides.title: Fragment Example
slides.content: Compare with and without a fragment:

quizData.question: Using a fragment (`<>...</>`) adds an extra element to the DOM.
quizData.explanation: False. Fragments do not create any additional DOM element; they simply group children.
